reviews.review_description: I like what the dev has done with this. He has taken a different approach to other cricket games. The environment is a beautiful picturesque village ground captured with a 360 photo. It works really well especially in conjunction with the organic sounds. Instead of facing a janky animated bowler you are up agaisnt a real person captured via video and green screen I presume. It all makes for a very compelling immersive cricket experience. The physics are good and bat tracking OK. UI and features are bare bones. Would be great if the game could be fleshed out more in the future.

reviews.review_title: Basic Gameplay is Good, but Barebones
reviews.review_description: I was trying to find other cricket vr games besides IB cricket, and this game is not bad. You see a real bowler bowling against you, and if you can calibrate your bat properly with your controller, doing net practice like session may feel nice. But menu system seems confusing, I have to exit the game and start again to select a new type of bowler. Also, I wish I could change the control as I mistakenly press palm button and new ball starts. I think this game is still at the demo stage, and it needs a lot of improvement.
